prompt:
Validate the current precision-corpus artifacts only; do not change product code or existing JSON artifacts. Write a concise report to research/codemap-precision/current-validation.txt.

Inputs:
- research/codemap-precision/current-source-report.json
- research/codemap-precision/space-rocks-precision-sample-150.json
- research/codemap-precision/curation-current/input-01.json through input-09.json

Checks:
1. Confirm the 150 sample pairs all exist in current-source-report.json with exact score/evidence payloads.
2. Confirm the nine inputs jointly cover exactly those 150 pairs once, with unchanged immutable fields and consistent metadata; report counts per input.
3. Confirm every sample target exists at Space Rocks revision 3387c94d10fdb94008f27b404098f3e0c32d911c, or identify missing targets.
4. Run focused tests for the current-suggestion and precision-sampling code.
5. Include the exact hackathon log path and thread ID.
No commit.

LoadSuggestionReport(reader io.Reader) (codemapbench.Report, error) {\\r\\n\\tvar envelope struct {\\r\\n\\t\\tSchemaVersion int `json:\\\"schema_version\\\"`\\r\\n\\t\\tcodemapbench.Report\\r\\n\\t}\\r\\n\\tif err := json.NewDecoder(reader).Decode(&envelope); err != nil {\\r\\n\\t\\treturn codemapbench.Report{}, suggestions and assigns\\r\\n// their deterministic rank within each document. suggestion)\\r\\n\\t}\\r\\n\\tresult := make([]Candidate, 0, len(report.UnmatchedSuggestions))\\r\\n\\tfor document, suggestions := range byDocument {\\r\\n\\t\\tsort.Slice(suggestions, func(i, j int) bool {\\r\\n\\t\\t\\tif suggestions[i].Score != suggestions[j].Score {\\r\\n\\t\\t\\t\\treturn suggestions[i].Score > suggestions[j].Score\\r\\n\\t\\t\\t}\\r\\n\\t\\t\\treturn suggestions[i].Target < suggestions[j].Target\\r\\n\\t\\t})\\r\\n\\t\\tfor index, suggestion := range suggestions {\\r\\n\\t\\t\\tresult = append(result, decorateCandidate(suggestion, index+1))\\r\\n\\t\\t}\\r\\n\\t}\\r\\n\\tsort.Slice(result, func(i, j int) bool {\\r\\n\\t\\tif result[i].Document != result[j].Document {\\r\\n\\t\\t\\treturn result[i].Document < result[j].Document\\r\\n\\t\\t}\\r\\n\\t\\treturn result[i].Rank < result[j].Rank\\r\\n\\t})\\r\\n\\treturn result\\r\\n}\\r\\n\\r\\nfunc Sample(candidates []Candidate, config SampleConfig) ([]LabeledSuggestion, error) {\\r\\n\\tif config.RequestedCount <= 0 {\\r\\n\\t\\treturn nil, errors.New(\\\"precision sample count must be positive\\\")\\r\\n\\t}\\r\\n\\tseed := strings.TrimSpace(config.Seed)\\r\\n\\tif seed == \\\"\\\" {\\r\\n\\t\\treturn nil, errors.New(\\\"precision sample seed is required\\\")\\r\\n\\t}\\r\\n\\tunique := uniqueCandidates(candidates)\\r\\n\\tif config.RequestedCount > len(unique) {\\r\\n\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"precision sample count %d exceeds %d candidates\\\", config.RequestedCount, len(unique))\\r\\n\\t}\\r\\n\\tselected := map[string]Candidate{}\\r\\n\\tbyDocument := map[string][]Candidate{}\\r\\n\\tfor _, candidate := range unique {\\r\\n\\t\\tbyDocument[candidate.Document] = append(byDocument[candidate.Document], candidate)\\r\\n\\t}\\r\\n\\t// Preserve every document's top five whenever the source has five entries.\\r\\n\\tfor document, values := range byDocument {\\r\\n\\t\\tsort.Slice(values, func(i, j int) bool { return values[i].Rank < values[j].Rank })\\r\\n\\t\\tlimit := 5\\r\\n\\t\\tif len(values) < limit {\\r\\n\\t\\t\\tlimit = len(values)\\r\\n\\t\\t}\\r\\n\\t\\tfor _, candidate := range values[:limit] {\\r\\n\\t\\t\\tselected[candidateKey(candidate)] = candidate\\r\\n\\t\\t}\\r\\n\\t\\tbyDocument[document] = values\\r\\n\\t}\\r\\n\\r\\n\\tremaining := make([]Candidate, 0, len(unique)-len(selected))\\r\\n\\tfor _, candidate := range unique {\\r\\n\\t\\tif _, ok := selected[candidateKey(candidate)]; !ok {\\r\\n\\t\\t\\tremaining = append(remaining, candidate)\\r\\n\\t\\t}\\r\\n\\t}\\r\\n\\tsort.Slice(remaining, func(i, j int) bool {\\r\\n\\t\\tleft := sampleHash(seed, remaining[i])\\r\\n\\t\\tright := sampleHash(seed, remaining[j])\\r\\n\\t\\tif left != right {\\r\\n\\t\\t\\treturn left < right\\r\\n\\t\\t}\\r\\n\\t\\treturn candidateKey(remaining[i]) < candidateKey(remaining[j])\\r\\n\\t})\\r\\n\\t// Fill by the least represented composite stratum. This keeps the sample\\r\\n\\t// deterministic while preventing the largest evidence family from\\r\\n\\t// monopolizing the non-mandatory portion.\\r\\n\\tfor len(selected) < config.RequestedCount {\\r\\n\\t\\tbestIndex := -1\\r\\n\\t\\tbestScore := \\\"\\\"\\r\\n\\t\\tcounts := map[string]int{}\\r\\n\\t\\tfor _, value := range selected {\\r\\n\\t\\t\\tcounts[stratum(value)]++\\r\\n\\t\\t}\\r\\n\\t\\tfor index, candidate := range remaining {\\r\\n\\t\\t\\tif _, ok := selected[candidateKey(candidate)]; ok {\\r\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\r\\n\\t\\t\\t}\\r\\n\\t\\t\\tscore := fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%08d\\\\x00%s\\\", counts[stratum(candidate)], sampleHash(seed+\\\"\\\\x00fill\\\", candidate))\\r\\n\\t\\t\\tif bestIndex < 0 || score < bestScore {\\r\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tbestIndex, bestScore = index, score\\r\\n\\t\\t\\t}\\r\\n\\t\\t}\\r\\n\\t\\tif bestIndex < 0 {\\r\\n\\t\\t\\tbreak\\r\\n\\t\\t}\\r\\n\\t\\tcandidate := remaining[bestIndex]\\r\\n\\t\\tselected[candidateKey(candidate)] = candidate\\r\\n\\t}\\r\\n\\tresult := make([]LabeledSuggestion, 0, len(selected))\\r\\n\\tfor _, candidate := range selected {\\r\\n\\t\\tresult = append(result, LabeledSuggestion{\\r\\n\\t\\t\\tSuggestion: candidate.Suggestion, Rank: candidate.Rank, Area: candidate.Area,\\r\\n\\t\\t\\tSubsystem: candidate.Subsystem, ScoreBucket: candidate.ScoreBucket,\\r\\n\\t\\t\\tRankBucket: candidate.RankBucket, PrimaryEvidenceKind: candidate.PrimaryEvidenceKind,\\r\\n\\t\\t\\tEvidenceKinds: append([]string(nil), candidate.EvidenceKinds...),\\r\\n\\t\\t})\\r\\n\\t}\\r\\n\\tsort.Slice(result, func(i, j int) bool {\\r\\n\\t\\tif result[i].Document != result[j].Document {\\r\\n\\t\\t\\treturn result[i].Document < result[j].Document\\r\\n\\t\\t}\\r\\n\\t\\treturn result[i].Rank < result[j].Rank\\r\\n\\t})\\r\\n\\treturn result, nil\\r\\n}\\r\\n\\r\\nfunc ValidateBenchmark(benchmark Benchmark) error {\\r\\n\\tif benchmark.SchemaVersion != SchemaVersion {\\r\\n\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"unsupported precision benchmark schema %d\\\", benchmark.SchemaVersion)\\r\\n\\t}\\r\\n\\tseen := map[string]struct{}{}\\r\\n\\tfor index, item := range benchmark.Suggestions {\\r\\n\\t\\tif !item.Label.Valid() {\\r\\n\\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"suggestion %d has invalid label %q\\\", index, item.Label)\\r\\n\\t\\t}\\r\\n\\t\\tif item.Document == \\\"\\\" || item.Target == \\\"\\\" || item.Rank < 1 {\\r\\n\\t\\t\\treturn {\\r\\n\\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"duplicate suggestion %s -> %s\\\", item.Document, item.Target)\\r\\n\\t\\t}\\r\\n\\t\\tseen[key] = struct{}{}\\r\\n\\t\\tif item.Rationale == \\\"\\\" || }\\r\\n\\tsort.Strings(keys)\\r\\n\\treturn keys\\r\\n}\\r\\n\\r\\n// TargetFingerprint is used by curation tooling to detect target drift.\\r\\nfunc TargetFingerprint(content []byte) string codemapPrecisionHelp(w io.Writer) {\\r\\n\\tfmt.Fprintln(w, \\\"usage: ddocs codemap precision [-h] --benchmark PATH --suggestions PATH [--format {text,json}] [--output PATH]\\\\n\\\\nEvaluate a labeled precision benchmark JSON\\\\n  --suggestions PATH    source codemap benchmark report JSON\\\\n  --format {text,json}  report format (default text)\\\\n  --output PATH         write the report \\\"ddocs codemap precision: error: %v\\\\n\\\", err)\\r\\n\\t\\treturn 2\\r\\n\\t}\\r\\n\\tif fs.NArg() != 0 || !benchmarkPath.set || !suggestionsPath.set {\\r\\n\\t\\tfmt.Fprintln(errOut, \\\"ddocs {"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_16","type":"command_execution","command":""C:\\Program Files\\WindowsApps\\Microsoft.PowerShell_7.6.3.0_x64__8wekyb3d8bbwe\\pwsh.exe" -Command "Get-Content research/codemap-precision/README.md; Write-Output '--- validation-b ---'; Get-Content research/codemap-precision/validation-b.md; Write-Output '--- validation-a ---'; Get-Content research/codemap-precision/validation-a.txt"","aggregated_output":"# Space Rocks codemap precision benchmark

This benchmark measures the quality of ordinary missing-link suggestions from the current Demon Docs pipeline. It complements, and does not modify, the positive-only recall benchmark in [`research/codemap-review/space-rocks-trusted-links.json`](../codemap-review/space-rocks-trusted-links.json).

## Corpus and sampling

- Corpus: Space Rocks revision `3387c94d10fdb94008f27b404098f3e0c32d911c` (10 reviewed documents, 51 trusted links).
- Source run: all 51 trusted links were hidden with seed `space-rocks-precision-v1`; the run produced 267 unique suggestions, including 24 recovered trusted links and 243 ordinary unmatched suggestions.
- Sample: 150 unique document-target pairs in [`space-rocks-precision-benchmark.json`](space-rocks-precision-benchmark.json).
- Sampling: every document’s top five was retained where available; the remainder was selected deterministically with SHA-256 least-represented fill across document area, score bucket, primary evidence kind, and rank bucket.
- Labels were assigned after inspecting the referenced document section and target file or directory. Each row records document and target references, excerpts, target kind, a target fingerprint, evidence, and a concise rationale.

The three labels are mutually exclusive: `valid_missing_link` is correct for strict precision, `plausible_but_unnecessary` is accepted only for acceptance precision, and `incorrect` is junk.

## Reproduction

Generate the source suggestion report without changing Space Rocks:

```text
go run ./cmd/ddocs codemap benchmark --repo D:\!bin\space-rocks --trusted-links research\codemap-review\space-rocks-trusted-links.json --holdout-count 51 --seed space-rocks-precision-v1 --format json --output tmp\codemap-precision\full-hidden.json
```

Evaluate the labeled sample:

```text
go run ./cmd/ddocs codemap precision --benchmark research\codemap-precision\space-rocks-precision-benchmark.json --suggestions tmp\codemap-precision\full-hidden.json
```

The evaluator verifies that every sampled pair, score/evidence payload, and rank still exists in the source report before calculating metrics.

## Results

Evaluation date: 2026-07-19.

| metric | result |
| --- | ---: |
| sample size | 150 |
| valid missing link | 98 |
| plausible but unnecessary | 26 |
| incorrect | 26 |
| overall precision | 65.33% |
| acceptance precision | 82.67% |
| precision@1 | 80.00% |
| precision@3 | 73.33% |
| precision@5 | 74.00% |

### Precision@k by document

| document | @1 | @3 | @5 |
| --- | ---: | ---: | ---: |
| data-sync-and-ssot-pipeline | 0.00% | 0.00% | 0.00% |
| observability-contract | 100.00% | 66.67% | 80.00% |
| devtools-window | 100.00% | 100.00% | 100.00% |
| spawn-tools | 100.00% | 100.00% | 100.00% |
| player-data-http-api | 100.00% | 100.00% | 80.00% |
| realtime-websocket-protocol | 100.00% | 100.00% | 100.00% |
| websocket-connection-lifecycle | 100.00% | 100.00% | 100.00% |
| diagnostic-aggregator runtime-and-report-flow | 0.00% | 0.00% | 0.00% |
| player-respawn | 100.00% | 66.67% | 80.00% |
| profile-stats-flow | 100.00% | 100.00% | 100.00% |

### Breakdowns

| primary evidence kind | n | valid | accepted | precision | acceptance |
| --- | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: |
| declared_symbol_mention | 58 | 55 | 58 | 94.83% | 100.00% |
| exact_path_mention | 52 | 42 | 49 | 80.77% | 94.23% |
| unique_basename_mention | 40 | 1 | 17 | 2.50% | 42.50% |

| score bucket | n | valid | accepted | precision | acceptance |
| --- | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: |
| `<1` | 13 | 1 | 2 | 7.69% | 15.38% |
| `1-<2` | 69 | 35 | 55 | 50.72% | 79.71% |
| `2-<8` | 39 | 34 | 38 | 87.18% | 97.44% |
| `8+` | 29 | 28 | 29 | 96.55% | 100.00% |

| rank bucket | n | valid | accepted | precision | acceptance |
| --- | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: |
| 1-5 | 50 | 37 | 46 | 74.00% | 92.00% |
| 6-10 | 33 | 22 | 30 | 66.67% | 90.91% |
| 11-20 | 34 | 20 | 26 | 58.82% | 76.47% |
| 21+ | 33 | 19 | 22 | 57.58% | 66.67% |

### Sampling coverage

The sample covers all 10 documents, all four score buckets, all four rank buckets, all three observed primary evidence kinds, and four document areas: data (26), devtools (41), protocol (33), and services (50). Subsystem counts are recorded in the evaluator’s JSON output and the row-level artifact.

## Limitations

This is a curated sample, not an unbiased estimate over every repository path. It is drawn from a full-hidden run of the 51-link trusted corpus, so it evaluates the current evidence pipeline under that benchmark setup and does not measure recall or unseen-document behavior. Directory suggestions are retained as ordinary candidates but are judged conservatively. Labels are auditable and source-pinned, but this artifact has one review pass rather than independent inter-rater agreement. The existing recall benchmark remains the appropriate measure for recovery of known links.

func TestFilterCodemapDatasetExcludesDocumentPrefixes(t *testing.T) {
	dataset := codemap.Dataset{

		},
		Diagnostics: []codemap.Diagnostic{
			{DocumentPath: "docs/keep.md"},
			{DocumentPath: ".worktrees/branch/docs/drop.md"},
		},
	}

	filtered := filterCodemapDataset(dataset, []string{`.worktrees\`})
	if len(filtered.Documents) != 1 || filtered.Documents[0].Path != "docs/keep.md" {
		t.Fatalf("documents = %#v", filtered.Documents)
	}

		t.Fatalf("entries = %#v", filtered.Entries)
	}
	if len(filtered.Diagnostics) != 1 || filtered.Diagnostics[0].DocumentPath != "docs/keep.md" {
		t.Fatalf("diagnostics = %#v", filtered.Diagnostics)
	}
}

package app

func TestCodemapPrecisionEvaluatesJSONBenchmark(t *testing.T) {
	withWorkingDirectory(t, t.TempDir(), func(root string) {
		benchmark := `{"schema_version":1,"suggestions":[{"document":"docs/a.md","target":"src/a.go","score":2,"evidence":["exact_path_mention:src/a.go"],"rank":1,"area":"test","subsystem":"test","score_bucket":"2-<8","rank_bucket":"1-5","primary_evidence_kind":"exact_path_mention","evidence_kinds":["exact_path_mention"],"label":"valid_missing_link","rationale":"reviewed","audit":{"document_ref":"docs/a.md:1","document_excerpt":"doc","target_ref":"src/a.go:1","target_excerpt":"code"}}]}`
		report := `{"schema_version":1,"unmatched_suggestions":[{"document":"docs/a.md","target":"src/a.go","score":2,"evidence":["exact_path_mention:src/a.go"]}]}`
		benchmarkPath, reportPath := filepath.Join(root, "benchmark.json"), filepath.Join(root, "report.json")
		if err := os.WriteFile(benchmarkPath, []byte(benchmark), 0o644); err != nil {
			t.Fatal(err)
		}
		if err := os.WriteFile(reportPath, []byte(report), 0o644); err != nil {
			t.Fatal(err)
		}
		var stdout, stderr bytes.Buffer
		code := Run(context.Background(), []string{"codemap", "precision", "--benchmark", benchmarkPath, "--suggestions", reportPath}, &stdout, &stderr)
		if code != 0 || stderr.Len() != 0 || !strings.Contains(stdout.String(), "Overall precision: 100.00%") {
			t.Fatalf("code=%d stdout=%q stderr=%q", code, stdout.String(), stderr.String())
		}
	})
}

func TestCodemapPrecisionSamplesUnlabeledTemplate(t *testing.T) {
	withWorkingDirectory(t, t.TempDir(), func(root string) {
		reportPath := filepath.Join(root, "report.json")
		report := `{"schema_version":1,"unmatched_suggestions":[{"document":"docs/a.md","target":"src/a.go","score":2,"evidence":["exact_path_mention:src/a.go"]}]}`
		if err := os.WriteFile(reportPath, []byte(report), 0o644); err != nil {
			t.Fatal(err)
		}
		var stdout, stderr bytes.Buffer
		code := Run(context.Background(), []string{"codemap", "precision", "sample", "--suggestions", reportPath, "--count", "1"}, &stdout, &stderr)
		if code != 0 || stderr.Len() != 0 {
			t.Fatalf("code=%d stdout=%q stderr=%q", code, stdout.String(), stderr.String())
		}
		var sample struct {
			Suggestions []struct {
				Label     string `json:"label"`
				Rationale string `json:"rationale"`
			} `json:"suggestions"`
		}
		if err := json.Unmarshal(stdout.Bytes(), &sample); err != nil {
			t.Fatal(err)
		}
		if len(sample.Suggestions) != 1 || sample.Suggestions[0].Label != "" || sample.Suggestions[0].Rationale != "" {
			t.Fatalf("unexpected sample: %#v", sample)
		}
	})

# Space Rocks codemap precision benchmark

This benchmark measures the quality of ordinary missing-link suggestions from the current Demon Docs pipeline. It complements, and does not modify, the positive-only recall benchmark in [`research/codemap-review/space-rocks-trusted-links.json`](../codemap-review/space-rocks-trusted-links.json).

## Corpus and sampling

- Corpus: Space Rocks revision `3387c94d10fdb94008f27b404098f3e0c32d911c` (10 reviewed documents, 51 trusted links).
- Source run: all 51 trusted links were hidden with seed `space-rocks-precision-v1`; the run produced 267 unique suggestions, including 24 recovered trusted links and 243 ordinary unmatched suggestions.
- Sample: 150 unique document-target pairs in [`space-rocks-precision-benchmark.json`](space-rocks-precision-benchmark.json).
- Sampling: every document’s top five was retained where available; the remainder was selected deterministically with SHA-256 least-represented fill across document area, score bucket, primary evidence kind, and rank bucket.
- Labels were assigned after inspecting the referenced document section and target file or directory. Each row records document and target references, excerpts, target kind, a target fingerprint, evidence, and a concise rationale.

The three labels are mutually exclusive: `valid_missing_link` is correct for strict precision, `plausible_but_unnecessary` is accepted only for acceptance precision, and `incorrect` is junk.

## Reproduction

Generate the source suggestion report without changing Space Rocks:

```text
go run ./cmd/ddocs codemap benchmark --repo D:\!bin\space-rocks --trusted-links research\codemap-review\space-rocks-trusted-links.json --holdout-count 51 --seed space-rocks-precision-v1 --format json --output tmp\codemap-precision\full-hidden.json
```

Evaluate the labeled sample:

```text
go run ./cmd/ddocs codemap precision --benchmark research\codemap-precision\space-rocks-precision-benchmark.json --suggestions tmp\codemap-precision\full-hidden.json
```

The evaluator verifies that every sampled pair, score/evidence payload, and rank still exists in the source report before calculating metrics.

Build a substantially better precision benchmark for the codemap missing-link suggestion algorithm, using Space Rocks at D:\!bin\space-rocks as the evaluation corpus. Do not merely describe a plan; implement and complete it.

Context:
- Current positive-only benchmark: research/codemap-review/space-rocks-trusted-links.json, 51 known-good links across 10 docs.
- Current holdout benchmark is useful for recall but reports misleadingly low apparent precision because unknown suggestions are counted false.
- Current tuned algorithm has around 100% recall on the default 10-link fold and ~3.45% apparent precision.
- We need a curated, traceable precision benchmark that labels ordinary suggestions.

Required outcome:
1. Inspect the current benchmark, CLI, evidence, report, and research artifacts.
2. Run the current suggestion pipeline against Space Rocks and create a deterministic stratified sample of at least 150 suggestions. Stratify across score/rank buckets, evidence types, document areas/subsystems, and include top-1/top-3/top-5 suggestions per document where possible. Avoid duplicate document-target pairs.
3. Curate each sampled suggestion into one of exactly:
   - valid_missing_link
   - plausible_but_unnecessary
   - incorrect
   Include concise, evidence-based rationale and enough source metadata to audit the label. Inspect the referenced document and target code, not just filenames. Do not silently infer labels from existing codemap inclusion alone.
4. Store the benchmark as a deterministic, reviewable JSON artifact under research/codemap-precision/ and add a concise methodology/report Markdown file there.
5. Add or extend Go tooling so the labeled benchmark can be evaluated reproducibly. Report at minimum:
   - overall precision where only valid_missing_link counts correct
   - acceptance precision where valid_missing_link + plausible_but_unnecessary count non-junk
   - precision@1, precision@3, precision@5 per document
   - breakdown by evidence kind and score/rank bucket
   - label counts and sampling coverage
6. Add focused tests for parsing, deterministic sampling/evaluation, and metric calculations.
7. Run gofmt and go test ./... . Run the real evaluation against the created benchmark and include the resulting metrics in the Markdown report.
8. Commit all finished work on the current branch with a focused commit message. Do not push.

Constraints:
- Preserve the existing 51-link recall benchmark.
- Do not modify Space Rocks.
- Do not loosen the suggestion algorithm just to improve the benchmark.
- Do not fabricate source inspection. Every label needs an auditable rationale grounded in the document and code target.
- Keep generated scratch output out of the commit.
- If the repo is dirty at start, preserve unrelated work and do not overwrite it; report any conflict.
- Use the existing project conventions and avoid broad unrelated cleanup.

At the end, report: changed files, commit hash, test results, benchmark size and label distribution, overall precision, acceptance precision, precision@1/@3/@5, and any limitations.
